tweets.text:
One of the lesser known advantages of prop accounts. 

When the markets are extremely volatile you use your prop account to participate with confidence because your risk is fixed at the eval cost. 

Lots of people taking advantage of this down turn to get funded. 

If you want to give use a try use code MAYNE at checkout for a discount.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
$1.2B got liquidated yesterday.
A bloodbath for most. Not all.

At Breakout it was the biggest funded day of the week.

100+ accounts funded on the dump. 
One payout cleared $66,000.

Volatility cuts both ways. Funded trading lets you play both sides with your downside capped.
tweets.full_text:
One of the lesser known advantages of prop accounts. 

When the markets are extremely volatile you use your prop account to participate with confidence because your risk is fixed at the eval cost.

tweets.text: Challenger reported U.S.-based employers announced 97,006 job cuts in May, the highest May total since the 2020 pandemic, up 16% from the 83,387 job cuts recorded in April. Laid-off workers will have a hard time finding new jobs just as inflation sends the cost of living soaring.

thread_tweets.text:
this anime reminds me that:

maybe noble prizes are cool, solving world hunger or inventing electricity or some shit

but because mental difficulties exist, there is no achievement in this world that is greater than overcoming yourself

tweets.text:
but maybe the smartest/driven people arent truly the smartest because if they were they'd find a way to dumb down

cat use to be smarter, and 9/10 happy, now am retarded but 10/10 happy, i guess what am tryna say is that i had a perspective, not that my pov = correct tho
